steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: mPulse is the suggestion box at every storefront. Every visitor leaves a note about how their visit went — and you read all of them at once.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
mPulse is RUM (real-user monitoring). A small JS beacon collects:

- Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID/INP, CLS)
- Page load timings (TTFB, DOM ready)
- Custom timers and metadata
- User context (geo, ASN, device, browser)

Data lands in dashboards within seconds. Often bundled free with Ion.

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Pair mPulse (real users) with synthetic monitoring (lab conditions) for full coverage. Use the alert engine to catch regressions per-route. Export raw beacons via DataStream for custom analytics.
"mPulse = real users telling you how the site actually feels. Pair with synthetic for full coverage."

quiz.question: Difference between RUM and synthetic monitoring?

options.text: RUM = real users; Synthetic = scripted lab tests
options.explain: RUM tells you what users actually experience. Synthetic tells you what a controlled test sees. Use both.
RUM works on any browser-served page.
Synthetic is more controlled, not more accurate. Real users are the truth.

quiz.question: Which Core Web Vital measures interactivity?

options.explain: LCP measures rendering speed, not interactivity.

options.text: INP — Interaction to Next Paint (replaced FID)
options.explain: INP is Google's replacement for FID. Measures the responsiveness of interactions across the whole page lifecycle.
CLS measures visual stability, not interactivity.
TTFB is server response time, not user interactivity.
